title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery Queen feels uneasy as the investigation appears to be winding down without a resolution, specifically questioning Crosetto's motives. While various characters—including Mrs. Bradley, Jonathan Lincoln, Helen Bradley, Hester Lincoln, and Dr. Temple—react to the ongoing tension, Andrew Van remains hidden in the West Virginia mountains and Stephen Megalos stays on his yacht. A telegram from Scotland Yard indicates that investigations into Percy and Elizabeth Rein in England have yielded no results, suggesting a need for coordination with European law enforcement.

On a Thursday morning, Ellery and Officer Vane discuss the lack of progress. While Fox is mowing the lawn, he suddenly stops and runs toward the trees after a scream is heard coming from the direction of the Rein residence. Ellery, Vane, and Fox rush to the scene and find Dr. Temple lying unconscious with a deep gash on his forehead. They carry him into the Rein house, which appears to have been ransacked, while Helen Bradley assists in tending to the injured doctor.

Officer Vane concludes that the Reins have fled the scene and contacts the authorities to report both the assault and the Reins' disappearance. As Dr. Temple regains consciousness, he reveals that he was attempting to crawl toward the house before blacking out. He then discloses a significant discovery: he believes the Reins are actually a couple known as the Tracetons. Temple recalls a theft of valuable gems in Budapest involving a German jeweler named Bondrein, and he identifies Percy Rein as the man he saw in Europe following his release from an Austrian prisoner-of-war camp.
